name: Acolyte
description: You devoted yourself to service in a temple, either nestled in a town or secluded in a sacred grove. There you performed rites in honor of a god or pantheon. You served under a priest and studied religion. Thanks to your priest's instruction and your own devotion, you also learned how to channel a modicum of divine power in service to your place of worship and the people who prayed there.

name: Artisan
description: You began mopping floors and scrubbing counters in an artisan's workshop for a few coppers per day as soon as you were strong enough to carry a bucket. When you were old enough to apprentice, you learned to create basic crafts of your own, as well as how to sweet-talk the occasional demanding customer. Your trade has also given you a keen eye for detail.

children.target: tool NOT gamingSet NOT musicalInstrument NOT thievesTools NOT poisonersKit NOT navigatorsTools NOT herbalismKit NOT forgeryKit NOT disguiseKit OR artisansToolsProficiency
children.name: Artisan's Tools Proficiency

name: Charlatan
description: Once you were old enough to order an ale, you soon had a favorite stool in every tavern within ten miles of where you were born. As you traveled the circuit from public house to watering hole, you learned to prey on unfortunates who were in the market for a comforting lie or two—perhaps a sham potion or forged ancestry records.

name: Criminal
description: You eked out a living in dark alleyways, cutting purses or burgling shops. Perhaps you were part of a small gang of like-minded wrongdoers who looked out for each other. Or maybe you were a lone wolf, fending for yourself against the local thieves' guild and more fearsome lawbreakers.

name: Entertainer
description: You spent much of your youth following roving fairs and carnivals, performing odd jobs for musicians and acrobats in exchange for lessons. You may have learned how to walk a tightrope, how to play a lute in a distinct style, or how to recite poetry with impeccable diction. To this day, you thrive on applause and long for the stage.

name: Farmer
description: You grew up close to the land. Years tending animals and cultivating the earth rewarded you with patience and good health. You have a keen appreciation for nature's bounty alongside a healthy respect for nature's wrath.

name: Guard
description: Your feet ache when you remember the countless hours you spent at your post in the tower. You were trained to keep one eye looking outside the wall, watching for marauders sweeping from the nearby forest, and your other eye looking inside the wall, searching for cutpurses and troublemakers.

name: Guide
description: You came of age outdoors, far from settled lands. Your home was anywhere you chose to spread your bedroll. There are wonders in the wilderness—strange monsters, pristine forests and streams, overgrown ruins of great halls once trod by giants—and you learned to fend for yourself as you explored them. From time to time, you guided friendly nature priests who instructed you in the fundamentals of channeling the magic of the wild.

name: Hermit
description: You spent your early years secluded in a hut or monastery located well beyond the outskirts of the nearest settlement. In those days, your only companions were the creatures of the forest and those who would occasionally visit to bring news of the outside world and supplies. The solitude allowed you to spend many hours pondering the mysteries of creation.

name: Merchant
description: You were apprenticed to a trader, caravan master, or shopkeeper, learning the fundamentals of commerce. You traveled broadly, and you earned a living by buying and selling the raw materials artisans need to practice their craft or finished works from such crafters. You might have transported goods from one place to another (by ship, wagon, or caravan) or bought them from traveling traders and sold them in your own shop.

name: Noble
description: You were raised in a castle, surrounded by wealth, power, and privilege. Your family of minor aristocrats ensured that you received a first-class education, some of which you appreciated and some of which you resented. Your time in the castle, especially the many hours you spent observing your family at court, also taught you a great deal about leadership.

name: Sage
description: You spent your formative years traveling between manors and monasteries, performing various odd jobs and services in exchange for access to their libraries. You whiled away many a long evening studying books and scrolls, learning the lore of the multiverse - even the rudiments of magic - and your mind yearns for more.

name: Sailor
description: You lived as a seafarer, wind at your back and decks swaying beneath your feet. You've perched on barstools in more ports of call than you can remember, faced mighty storms, and swapped stories with folk who live beneath the waves.

name: Scribe
description: You spent formative years in a scriptorium, a monastery dedicated to the preservation of knowledge, or a government agency, where you learned to write with a clear hand and produce finely written texts. Perhaps you scribed government documents or copied tomes of literature. You might have some skill as a writer of poetry, narrative, or scholarly research. Above all, you have a careful attention to detail, helping you avoid introducing mistakes to the documents you copy and create.

name: Soldier
description: You began training for war as soon as you reached adulthood and carry precious few memories of life before you took up arms. Battle is in your blood. Sometimes you catch yourself reflexively performing the basic fighting exercises you learned first. Eventually, you put that training to use on the battlefield, protecting the realm by waging war.

name: Wayfarer
description: You grew up on the streets surrounded by similarly ill-fated castoffs, a few of them friends and a few of them rivals. You slept where you could and did odd jobs for food. At times, when the hunger became unbearable, you resorted to theft. Still, you never lost your pride and never abandoned hope. Fate is not yet finished with you.

children.name: Armor
children.target: unarmored OR $(attributes.proficiencies.heavyArmor ? 'heavyArmor OR mediumArmor OR lightArmor' : attributes.proficiencies.mediumArmor ? 'mediumArmor OR lightArmor' : '$(attributes.proficiencies.lightArmor ? 'lightArmor' : '')')

children.name: Armament
children.target: simple $(attributes.proficiencies.shields ? 'OR shieldEquipment' : '') $(attributes.proficiencies.martialWeapons ? 'OR martial' : '') $(attributes.proficiencies.lightWeapons ? 'OR light' : '') $(attributes.proficiencies.finesseWeapons ? 'OR rapier OR whip' : '')

name: Artificer
description: Masters of invention, Artificers use ingenuity and magic to unlock extraordinary capabilities in objects. They see magic as a complex system waiting to be decoded and then harnessed in their spells and inventions.

children.name: Artificer Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR historyProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR natureProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR sleightOfHandProficiency
tool NOT gamingSet NOT musicalInstrument NOT thievesTools NOT poisonersKit NOT navigatorsTools NOT herbalismKit NOT forgeryKit NOT disguiseKit OR artisansToolsProficiency
children.name: Artisan's Tools Proficiency
Artificer Spells

children.name: Replicate Magic Item Spells
children.target: createFoodAndWater OR leomundsSecretChest OR waterBreathing OR darkvision OR fabricate OR spiritualWeapon OR ropeTrick OR sending OR shield OR detectMagic OR findTraps OR bless OR magicWeapon $(meta.level >= 6 ? 'OR mageArmor OR passWithoutTrace OR enhanceAbility OR foresight OR seeInvisibility OR fear OR thunderwave OR waterWalk' : '')

children.name: Flash of Genius
children.description: When you or a creature you can see within range fails an ability check or a saving throw, add $(Math.floor(stats.int.mod, 1)) to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed.

name: Barbarian
description: Barbarians are mighty warriors who are powered by primal forces of the multiverse that manifest as a Rage. More than a mere emotion—and not limited to anger—this Rage is an incarnation of a predator's ferocity, a storm's fury, and a sea's turmoil.

children.name: Barbarian Skill Proficiencies
children.target: animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR natureProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Rage
children.description:
You imbue yourself with primal power. While active, you gain the following benefits:

**Damage Resistance.** You have resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. 

**Rage Damage.** When you make an attack using Strength and deal damage, you gain a $(formatBonus(attributes.rageDamage)) bonus to the damage. 

**Strength Advantage.** You have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

children.name: Reckless Attack
children.description:
When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you attack recklessly. You gain Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

$(meta.level >= 9 ? "You can forgo Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll on your turn. If the chosen attack hits, the target takes an extra $(meta.level >= 17 ? '2d10' : '1d10') damage and you can cause $(meta.level > 17 ? 'two effects of your choice:' : 'one effect of your choice:')" : "")

children.name: Primal Knowledge Skill Proficiency
children.target: animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR natureProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Primal Knowledge
children.description: You can make ability checks using Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

children.name: Instinctive Pounce
children.description: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Rage, move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet.

name: Bard
description: Invoking magic through music, dance, and verse, Bards are expert at inspiring others, soothing hurts, disheartening foes, and creating illusions. Bards believe the multiverse was spoken into existence and that remnants of its Words of Creation still resound and glimmer on every plane of existence.

children.name: Bardic Inspiration
children.description:
You inspire another creature within range who can see or hear you. The creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice (d$(attributes.bardicDie)).

Once within the next hour when the creature fails a D20 Test, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning the failure into a success. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.

children.name: Bard Prepared Spells
children.target: $(meta.level >= 10 ? '(bard OR cleric OR druid OR wizard)' : 'bard')  $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '') NOT cantrip

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.name: Countercharm
children.description: When you or an ally within range fails a saving throw, against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you use musical notes or words of power to disrupt its mind-influencing effect. Cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has Advantage.

children.name: Force Demolisher
children.description:
Make a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + attributes.prof + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) attack. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 15 ? '2d6' : '1d10')$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) Force damage.

If you hit a creature that is at least one size smaller than you with the demolisher, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself or pull the creature up to 10 feet toward yourself.

children.name: Giant Stature
children.description:
As a Bonus Action, you transform and enlarge your armor for 1 minute. For the duration:
- Your reach increases by $(meta.level >= 15 ? '10' : '5') feet.
- If you are smaller than Large, you become Large (at level 15, you can choose to become Large or Huge).
- At level 15, you have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

children.name: Thunder Pulse
children.description:
Make a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + attributes.prof + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) attack. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 15 ? '1d10' : '1d8')$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) Thunder damage.

A creature hit by the pulse has Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Defensive Field
children.description: While Bloodied, you can gain ($(meta.level)) Temporary Hit Points. You lose these Temporary Hit Points if you doff the armor.

children.name: Perfected Guardian Reaction
children.description: When a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within range, you can magically force that creature to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you pull the creature up to 25 feet directly toward you to an unoccupied space. If you pull the target to a space within 5 feet of yourself, you can make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this Reaction.

children.name: Lightning Launcher
children.description:
Make a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + attributes.prof + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) attack. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 15 ? '2d6' : '1d6')$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0))) Lightning damage.

Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with the launcher, you can deal an extra 1d6 Lightning damage to that target.

$(meta.level >= 15 ? 'Any creature that takes Lightning damage from your Lightning Launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The glimmering creature sheds Dim Light in a 5-foot radius, and it has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, as the light jolts it if it attacks you.' : '')

children.name: Perfected Infiltrator Flight
children.description: You gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your Speed ($(2 * attributes.movement.walk) feet) until the end of the current turn.

children.name: Divine Strike
children.description: Once per turn, the target takes an extra $(meta.level >= 14 ? '2d8' : '1d8') Necrotic or Radiant damage.

children.name: Potent Spellcasting
children.description: On a hit, grant $(2 * stats.wis.mod) Temporary Hit Points to yourself or another creature within 60 feet.

children.target: activities[tags=cantrip AND tags=druid NOT range=self NOT range=touch].range

children.name: Primal Strike
children.description: On a hit, the target takes an additional $(meta.level >= 15 ? "2d8" : "1d8") Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage.

children.value: damage + 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning or Thunder damage

name: Ambush
description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an Initiative roll, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the roll, unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Bait and Switch
description: When you're within 5 feet of a creature on your turn, you switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 5 feet of movement and the creature is willing and doesn't have the Incapacitated condition. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) bonus to AC.

name: Commander's Strike
description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you replace one of your attacks to direct one of your companions to strike. Choose a willing creature who can see or hear you. That creature can immediately use its Reaction to make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, adding 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack's damage roll on a hit.

name: Commanding Presence
description: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion) check, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the roll.

name: Disarming Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you attempt to disarm the target. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.superioritySave) Strength saving throw or drop one object of your choice that it’s holding, with the object landing in its space.

name: Distracting Strike
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you distract the target. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has Advantage if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.

name: Evasive Footwork
description: You take the Disengage action and add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to your AC until the start of your next turn.

name: Feinting Attack
description: You feint, choosing one creature within 5 feet of yourself as your target. You have Advantage on your next attack roll against that target this turn. If that attack hits, add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll.

name: Goading Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you attempt to goad the target into attacking you. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.superioritySave) Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

name: Lunging Attack
description: You take the Dash action. If you move at least 5 feet in a straight line immediately before hitting with a melee attack as part of the Attack action on this turn, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll.

name: Maneuvering Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you maneuver one of your comrades into another position. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll, and choose a willing creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its Reaction to move up to half its Speed without provoking an Opportunity Attack from the target of your attack.

name: Menacing Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you attempt to frighten the target. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.superioritySave) Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

name: Parry
description: When another creature damages you with a melee attack roll, you reduce the damage by 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie)$(formatBonus(Math.max(1, stats.str.mod, stats.dex.mod))).

name: Precision Attack
description: When you miss with an attack roll, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit.

name: Pushing Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you attempt to drive the target back. Add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a DC $(attributes.superioritySave) Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet directly away from you.

name: Rally
description: You bolster the resolve of a companion. Choose an ally of yours within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie)+$(Math.floor(meta.level/2)) Temporary Hit Points.

name: Riposte
description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you make a melee attack roll with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against the creature. If you hit, add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage.

name: Sweeping Attack
description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you attempt to damage another creature. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie). The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

name: Tactical Assessment
description: When you make an Intelligence (History or Investigation) check or a Wisdom (Insight) check, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the ability check.

name: Trip Attack
description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you add 1d$(attributes.superiorityDie) to the attack’s damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a DC $(attributes.superioritySave) Strength saving throw or have the Prone condition.

name: Escape the Horde
description: Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you.

name: Multiattack Defense
description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

name: Colossus Slayer
description: On a hit, once per turn, if the target is missing any of its Hit Points, the target takes an extra 1d8 damage.

name: Horde Breaker
description: Once per turn, you can make another attack against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

children.name: Careful Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you protect some of those creatures from the spell’s full force. Choose a number of those creatures up to $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell, and it takes no damage if it would normally take half damage on a successful save.

children.name: Distant Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 5 feet, you double the spell’s range. Or when you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you make the spell’s range 30 feet.

children.name: Empowered Spell
children.description:
When you roll damage for a spell, you can reroll a number of the damage dice up to $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)), and you must use the new rolls.

You can use Empowered Spell even if you’ve already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

children.name: Extended Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you double its duration to a maximum duration of 24 hours. If the affected spell requires Concentration, you have Advantage on any saving throw you make to maintain that Concentration.

children.name: Heightened Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you give one target of the spell Disadvantage on saves against the spell.

children.name: Quickened Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you change the casting time to a Bonus Action for this casting. You can’t modify a spell in this way if you’ve already cast a level 1+ spell on the current turn, nor can you cast a level 1+ spell on this turn after modifying a spell in this way.

children.name: Seeking Spell
children.description:
If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll.

You can use Seeking Spell even if you’ve already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

children.name: Subtle Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell, you cast it without any Verbal, Somatic, or Material components, except Material components that are consumed by the spell or that have a cost specified in the spell.

children.name: Transmuted Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.

children.name: Twinned Spell
children.description: When you cast a spell, such as Charm Person, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, you increase the spell’s effective level by 1.

children.name: Eldritch Smite
children.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, the target takes an extra 1d8 Force damage, plus another 1d8 per level of the spell slot. You can knock the target Prone if it is Huge or smaller.

name: Gaze of Two Minds
description: Touch a willing creature to perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. Use a Bonus Action to extend the duration.

children.name: Command Familiar
children.description: You command the familiar to make an attack.
You can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own with its Reaction.
children.name: Pact of the Chain

name: Repelling Blast
description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature, you can push it up to 10 feet straight away from you.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

name: Cleric
description: Clerics draw power from the realms of the gods and harness it to work miracles. Blessed by a deity, a pantheon, or another immortal entity, a Cleric can reach out to the divine magic of the Outer Planes—where gods dwell—and channel it to bolster people and battle foes.

children.name: Cleric Skill Proficiencies
children.target: historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Cleric Prepared Spells
children.target: cleric NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.name: Divine Spark
children.description: You point your Holy Symbol at another creature you can see. Roll $(meta.level >= 18 ? '4d8' : meta.level >= 13 ? '3d8' : meta.level >= 7 ? '2d8' : '1d8')$(formatBonus(stats.wis.mod)). You either restore Hit Points to the creature equal to that total or force the creature to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice) equal to that total. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage (round down).

children.name: Turn Undead
children.description: You present your Holy Symbol and censure Undead creatures. Each Undead of your choice within range must make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its save, it has the Frightened and Incapacitated conditions. It tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns. The effect ends early if it takes any damage, if you have the Incapacitated condition, or if you die.

children.name: Sear Undead
children.description: Each Undead that fails its saving throw takes $(Math.max(1, stats.wis.mod))d8 Radiant damage. This damage doesn't end the turn effect.

name: Druid
description: Druids belong to ancient orders that call on the forces of nature. Harnessing the magic of animals, plants, and the four elements, Druids heal, transform into animals, and wield elemental destruction.

children.name: Druid Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR animalHandlingProficiency OR insightProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR natureProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR religionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Druid Spells

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.target: druid NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: Wild Shape
children.description:
You transform into a beast form. You choose whether your equipment falls to the ground, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:

**Temporary Hit Points.** You gain $(meta.level * 2) Temporary Hit Points.

**Game Statistics.** Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your creature type; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores.

**No Spellcasting.** You can't cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn't break your Concentration or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.

name: Fighter
description: Fighters rule many battlefields. Questing knights, royal champions, elite soldiers, and hardened mercenaries - as Fighters, they all share an unparalleled prowess with weapons and armor. And they are well acquainted with death, both meting it out and defying it.

children.name: Fighter Skill Proficiencies
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Second Wind
children.description: You draw on a well of physical and mental stamina. Regain 1d10+$(meta.level) Hit Points.

children.name: Action Surge
children.description: When you take an action, you push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. Take one additional action, except the Magic action.

children.name: Tactical Mind
children.description: When you fail an ability check, you push yourself toward success. Add 1d10 to the ability check, potentially turning it into a success. If the check still fails, this use of Second Wind isn’t expended.

children.name: Tactical Shift
children.description: You can move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack $(meta.level >= 20 ? 'four times' : meta.level >= 11 ? 'three times' : 'twice'), instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

name: Monk
description: Monks use rigorous combat training and mental discipline to align themselves with the multiverse and focus their internal reservoirs of power. Different Monks conceptualize this power in various ways: as breath, energy, life force, essence, or self, for example. Whether channeled as a striking display of martial prowess or as a subtler manifestation of defense and speed, this power infuses all that a Monk does.

children.name: Monk Skill Proficiencies
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR religionProficiency OR stealthProficiency

children.name: Monk Tool Proficiency
children.target: tool NOT gamingSet NOT thievesTools NOT poisonersKit NOT navigatorsTools NOT herbalismKit NOT forgeryKit NOT disguiseKit OR artisansToolsProficiency

children.name: Bonus Unarmed Strike
children.description: You make one Unarmed Strike.

children.name: Flurry of Blows
children.description: You make $(meta.level >= 10 ? 'three' : 'two') Unarmed Strikes.

children.name: Patient Defense
children.description: You take both the Disengage and Dodge actions$(meta.level >= 10 ? ' and gain ' + (2) + 'd' + attributes.martialArtsDie + ' Temporary Hit Points' : '').

children.name: Step of the Wind
children.description: You take both the Disengage and Dash actions, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.$(meta.level >= 10 ? ' You can also move a willing creature (Large or smaller) within 5 feet with you until the end of your turn without provoking Opportunity Attacks.' : '')

children.name: Deflect Attacks
children.description:
When an attack hits you and deals $(meta.level >= 13 ? 'any damage type' : 'Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage'), you reduce the total damage by 1d10+$(stats.dex.mod + meta.level). 

If you reduce the damage to 0, the Focus Point is expended and you redirect the force. Choose creature within $(meta.level >= 13 ? '60' : '5') feet. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Dexterity saving throw or take 2d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.dex.mod) damage of the same type.

children.name: Slow Fall
children.description: When you fall, you reduce any fall damage you take by $(meta.level * 5).

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

children.name: Stunning Strike
children.description: Once per turn when you hit with an attack, the target must make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn. On a success, the target's Speed is halved and the next attack roll against it has Advantage.

children.name: Empowered Strikes
children.description: You can deal your choice of Force damage or the normal damage type.

children.name: Evasion
children.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this if you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Paladin
description: Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of annihilation and corruption. Whether sworn before a god's altar, in a sacred glade before nature spirits, or in a moment of desperation and grief with the dead as the only witness, a Paladin's oath is a powerful bond. It is a source of power that turns a devout warrior into a blessed champion.

children.name: Paladin Skill Proficiencies
children.target: athleticsProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Lay On Hands
children.description: You touch a creature (which could be yourself) and restore 5 Hit Points to it per charge expended. $(meta.level >= 14 ? 'You can also expend a charge to remove one or more of the following conditions from the creature: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned.' : '')

children.name: Paladin Spells
children.target: paladin NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: Divine Sense
children.description: You open your awareness to detect Celestials, Fiends, and Undead. For the duration or until you have the Incapacitated condition, you know the location of any creature of those types within range, and you know its creature type. Within the same radius, you also detect the presence of any place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated, as with the Hallow spell.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

children.name: Aura of Protection
children.description: You radiate a protective, unseeable aura. The aura is inactive while you have the Incapacitated condition. You and your allies in the aura gain a $(formatBonus(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod))) bonus to saving throws.

name: Ranger
description: Far from bustling cities, amid the trees of trackless forests and across wide plains, Rangers keep their unending watch in the wilderness. Rangers learn to track their quarry as a predator does, moving stealthily through the wilds and hiding themselves in brush and rubble.

children.name: Ranger Skill Proficiencies
children.target: animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR insightProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR natureProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR stealthProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Ranger Spells
children.target: ranger NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

name: Rogue
description: Rogues rely on cunning, stealth, and their foes' vulnerabilities to get the upper hand in any situation. They have a knack for finding the solution to just about any problem. A few even learn magical tricks to supplement their other abilities. Many Rogues focus on stealth and deception, while others refine skills that help them in a dungeon environment, such as climbing, finding and disarming traps and opening locks.

children.name: Rogue Skill Proficiencies
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR deceptionProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR sleightOfHandProficiency OR stealthProficiency

children.name: Sneak Attack
children.description: Once per turn, you deal an extra $(attributes.sneakAttackDice)d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll or at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target.

children.name: Cunning Action
children.description: You take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

children.name: Steady Aim
children.description: You give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

children.name: Cunning Strike
children.description: When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can choose to spend dice from the Sneak Attack to create one of the following effects:

children.name: Poison (Cost: 1d6)
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.cunningStrikeSave) Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the poisoned target repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.

children.name: Trip (Cost: 1d6)
children.description: If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a DC $(attributes.cunningStrikeSave) Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition.

children.name: Withdraw (Cost: 1d6)
children.description: Immediately after the attack, move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

children.name: Uncanny Dodge
children.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you halve the attack's damage against you (round down).

children.name: Evasion
children.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this if you have the Incapacitated condition.

name: Sorcerer
description: Sorcerers wield innate magic that is stamped into their being. Some Sorcerers can’t name the origin of their power, while others trace it to strange events in their personal or family history. The blessing of a dragon or a dryad at a baby’s birth or the strike of lightning from a clear sky might spark a Sorcerer’s gift.

children.name: Sorcerer Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR deceptionProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Sorcerer Prepared Spells
children.target: sorcerer NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.name: Innate Sorcery
children.description:
You unleash simmering magic within yourself. For the duration, you gain the following benefits:
*   The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
*   You have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.
$(meta.level >= 7 ? "*   You can use up to two Metamagic options on each spell you cast." : "")

name: Alchemist
description: An Alchemist is an expert at combining reagents to produce magical effects. Alchemists use their creations to give life and to leech it away.

children.name: Experimental Elixir
children.description:
You drink the elixir or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of itself. The elixir has one of the following effects:

**1. Healing.** The drinker regains hit points equal to 2d8 plus your Intelligence modifier ($(2 + (meta.level >= 9 ? 1 : 0) + (meta.level >= 15 ? 1 : 0))d8$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod))).

**2. Swiftness.** The drinker’s Speed increases by $(meta.level >= 15 ? 20 : meta.level >= 9 ? 15 : 10) feet for 1 hour.

**3. Resilience.** The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for $(meta.level >= 15 ? '8 hours' : meta.level >= 9 ? '1 hour' : '10 minutes').

**4. Boldness.** The drinker rolls 1d4 and adds it to attack rolls and saving throws for $(meta.level >= 15 ? '1 hour' : meta.level >= 9 ? '10 minutes' : '1 minute').

**5. Flight.** The drinker gains a Fly Speed of $(meta.level >= 15 ? 30 : meta.level >= 9 ? 20 : 10) feet for 10 minutes.

**6. Choice.** Choose one of the above.

children.target: activities[(tags=damageSpell AND (tags=acidDamage OR tags=fireDamage OR tags=poisonDamage)) OR tags=healingSpell].description["d12"]
activities[(tags=damageSpell AND (tags=acidDamage OR tags=fireDamage OR tags=poisonDamage)) OR tags=healingSpell].description["d10"]
activities[(tags=damageSpell AND (tags=acidDamage OR tags=fireDamage OR tags=poisonDamage)) OR tags=healingSpell].description["d8"]
activities[(tags=damageSpell AND (tags=acidDamage OR tags=fireDamage OR tags=poisonDamage)) OR tags=healingSpell].description["d6"]
activities[(tags=damageSpell AND (tags=acidDamage OR tags=fireDamage OR tags=poisonDamage)) OR tags=healingSpell].description["d4"]

name: Armorer
description: An Armorer modifies armor to function almost like a second skin. The armor is enhanced to hone the Armorer’s magic, unleash potent attacks, and generate a formidable defense.

children.name: Tools of the Trade
children.description:
You gain training with Heavy armor.
You gain proficiency with Smith’s Tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of Artisan’s Tools of your choice.

When you craft nonmagical or magic armor, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

name: Artillerist
description: An Artillerist specializes in using magic to hurl energy, projectiles, and explosions on a battlefield.

children.name: Eldritch Cannon: Flamethrower
children.description: The cannon exhales fire in a 15-foot Cone. Each creature in that area makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw, taking $(meta.level >= 9 ? '3d8' : '2d8') Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Flammable objects in the Cone start burning.

children.name: Eldritch Cannon: Force Ballista
children.description: Make a $(formatBonus(stats.int.mod + attributes.prof)) attack originating from the cannon at one creature or object within range. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 9 ? '3d8' : '2d8') Force damage, and if the target is a creature, it is pushed up to 5 feet away from the cannon.

children.name: Eldritch Cannon: Protector
children.description: The cannon emits a burst of positive energy that grants itself and each creature of your choice within range gains $(meta.level >= 9 ? '2d8' : '1d8')$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) Temporary Hit Points.

name: Cartographer
description: Cartographers are the premier navigators and reconnaissance agents. Using their creations, Cartographers can highlight threats, safeguard allies, and carve portals to distant locations.

children.name: Adventurer's Atlas
children.description:
Whenever you finish a Long Rest while holding Cartographer’s Tools, you can use that tool to create a set of magical maps by touching at least two creatures (one of whom can be yourself), up to a maximum number of $(Math.max(1, stats.int.mod)) creatures.

While carrying the map, a target gains the following benefits:

**Awareness.** The target adds 1d4 to its Initiative rolls.

**Positioning.** The target knows the location of all other map holders that are on the same plane of existence as itself. When casting a spell or creating another effect that requires being able to see the effect’s target, a map holder can target another map holder regardless of sight or cover, so long as the other map holder is still within range.

children.name: Portal Jump
children.description: On your turn, you can spend $(Math.floor(attributes.movement.walk / 2)) feet of movement to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of yourself or within 5 feet of a creature that is within 30 feet of you and holding one of your Adventurer’s Atlas maps. You can’t use this benefit if your Speed is 0.

children.name: Guided Precision
children.description:
Once per turn, whenever you cast a spell from your Cartographer Spells list or hit a creature affected by your Faerie Fire with an attack roll, you can add $(Math.max(1, stats.int.mod)) to one damage roll of the spell or attack.

In addition, taking damage can’t cause you to lose Concentration on Faerie Fire.

name: Reanimator
description: Reanimators defy the laws of nature in pursuit of gruesome experiments. These grim Artificers stitch together servants from disparate corpses, use foul magic to strengthen the living, and transform the art of necromancy into a terrifying science.

children.name: Jolt to Life
children.description:
Revive a target with Spare the Dying. The target regains $(meta.level) Hit Points.

Additionally, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the target makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? '4d4' : meta.level >= 11 ? '3d4' : '2d4') Lightning damage, or half as much on a successful one.

name: Path of the Berserker
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Berserker direct their Rage primarily toward violence. Their path is one of untrammeled fury, and they thrill in the chaos of battle as they allow their Rage to seize and empower them.

children.name: Frenzy
children.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra $(attributes.rageDamage)d6 extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack.

name: Path of the Wild Heart
description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn magical means to communicate with animals, and their Rage heightens their connection to animals as it fills them with supernatural might.

children.name: Rage of the Wilds
children.description: Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. When you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice:

children.name: Bear
children.description: While your Rage is active, you have Resistance to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.

children.name: Eagle
children.description: When you activate your Rage, take the Disengage and Dash actions as part of that Bonus Action. While your Rage is active, you can take a Bonus Action to take both of those actions.

children.name: Wolf
children.description: While your Rage is active, your allies have Advantage on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 feet of you.

name: Path of the World Tree
description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree connect with the cosmic tree Yggdrasil through their Rage. This tree grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and the Material Plane. These Barbarians draw on the tree's magic for vitality and as a means of dimensional travel.

children.name: Vitality Surge
children.description: Gain $(meta.level) Temporary Hit Points.

children.name: Life-Giving Force
children.description: You tap into the life force of the World Tree. At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, choose a creature within range to gain $(attributes.rageDamage)d6 Temporary Hit Points. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

children.name: Branches of the Tree
children.description: When a creature you can see starts its turn within range while your Rage is active, you summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.str.mod + attributes.prof) Strength saving throw or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself. After it teleports, you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

name: Path of the Zealot
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a god or pantheon. These Barbarians experience their Rage as an ecstatic episode of divine union that infuses them with power. They are often allies to the priests and other followers of their god or pantheon.

children.name: Divine Fury
children.description: You channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit takes 1d6+$(Math.floor(meta.level/2)) extra damage. The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you deal the damage.

children.name: Warrior of the Gods
children.description: A divine entity helps ensure you can continue the fight. You regain 1d12 Hit Points per charge expended.

children.name: Fanatical Focus
children.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a $(formatBonus(attributes.rageDamage)) bonus. You must use the new roll.

name: College of Dance
description: Bards of the College of Dance know that the Words of Creation can't be contained within speech or song; the words are uttered by the movements of celestial bodies and flow through the motions of the smallest creatures. These bards practice a way of being in harmony with the whirling cosmos that emphasizes agility, speed, and grace.

children.name: Agile Strikes
children.description: You can make one Unarmed Strike as part of this action.

children.name: Inspiring Movement
children.description: When an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you, you move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Then one ally within 30 feet can also move up to half their Speed using their Reaction.

children.name: Tandem Footwork
children.description: When you roll Initiative, you and each ally within 30 feet gain a 1d$(attributes.bardicDie) bonus to Initiative.

name: College of Glamour
description: Bards of the College of Glamour trace their origins to the beguiling magic of the Feywild. These bards weave threads of beauty and terror into their songs and stories, and the mightiest among them can cloak themselves in otherworldly majesty.

children.name: Beguiling Magic
children.description: After casting an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot, you force a creature within 60 feet to make a Wisdom save. On a failure, it has the Charmed or Frightened condition (your choice).

children.name: Mantle of Inspiration
children.description: You provide $(2 * attributes.bardicDie) Temporary Hit Points to up to $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)) creatures within 60 feet. Each can use its Reaction to move up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

children.name: Mantle of Majesty
children.description:
You cast Command without expending a spell slot and assume an unearthly appearance for the duration. 

While active, you can cast Command as a Bonus Action without expending a spell slot. Any creature Charmed by you automatically fails its save against this Command.

name: College of Lore
description: Bards of the College of Lore collect spells and secrets from diverse sources, such as scholarly tomes, mystical rites, and peasant tales.

children.name: Cutting Words
children.description: When a creature that you can see makes a damage roll, ability check, or attack roll, you use your wit to suppernaturally distract, confuse or otherwise sap its confidence and competence. The target substracts 1d$(attributes.bardicDie) from the roll.

children.name: Magical Discoveries
children.target: (cleric OR druid OR wizard) NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

name: College of Valor
description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring storytellers whose tales preserve the memory of the great heroes of the past. These bards sing the deeds of the mighty in vaulted halls or to crowds gathered around great bonfires.

children.name: Combat Inspiration
children.description:
A creature with a Bardic Inspiration die from you can also use it in one of the following ways:

**Defense.** When hit by an attack roll, use a Reaction to roll the die and add the result to AC, potentially causing the attack to miss.

**Offense.** When hitting with an attack roll, use a Reaction to roll the die and add the result to the damage roll.

children.name: Extra Attack
children.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. You can replace one attack with a cantrip that has a casting time of an action.

name: Life Domain
description:
The Life Domain focuses on the positive energy that helps sustain all life in the multiverse. Clerics who tap into this domain are masters of healing, using that life force to cure many hurts.

Existence itself relies on the positive energy associated with this domain, so a Cleric of almost any religious tradition might choose it. This domain is particularly associated with agricultural deities, gods of healing or endurance, and gods of home and community. Religious orders of healing also seek the magic of this domain.

children.name: Disciple of Life
children.description: The creature regains an additional $(2+(stats.wis.mod)) Hit Points.

children.name: Preserve Life
children.description: You evoke healing energy to restore up to $(5 * meta.level) Hit Points. Choose Bloodied creatures within range and divide the points among them. This can't restore a creature to more than half its Hit Point maximum.

children.name: Blessed Healer
children.description: If you cast this on another creature, you regain $(2+(stats.wis.mod)) Hit Points.

name: Light Domain
description:
The Light Domain emphasizes the divine power to bring about blazing fire and revelation. Clerics who wield this power are enlightened souls infused with radiance and the power of their deities’ discerning vision, charged with chasing away lies and burning away darkness.

The Light Domain is associated with gods of truth, vigilance, beauty, insight, and renewal. Some of these gods are identified with the sun or as charioteers who guide the sun across the sky. Others are sentinels who pierce deception. Some are deities of beauty and artistry who teach that art is a vehicle for the soul’s improvement.

children.name: Radiance of the Dawn
children.description: You emit a flash of light in a 30-foot Emmanation originating from yourself. Any magical Darkness in the area is dispelled. Each creature of your choice in the area must make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw, taking $(2)d10+$(meta.level) Radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

children.name: Warding Flare
children.description:
When a creature that you can see within range makes an attack roll, you impose Disadvantage on the roll.
$(meta.level >= 6 ? "You also give the target of the attack $(2)d6$(formatBonus(stats.wis.mod)) Temporary Hit Points." : "")

name: Trickery Domain
description:
The Trickery Domain offers magic of deception, illusion, and stealth. Clerics who wield this magic are a disruptive force in the world, puncturing pride, mocking tyrants, freeing captives, and flouting hollow traditions. They prefer subterfuge and pranks to direct confrontation.

Gods of trickery are mischief-makers and instigators who stand as a constant challenge to the accepted order among both gods and mortals. They embody the forces of change and social upheaval, and they’re patrons of thieves, scoundrels, gamblers, rebels, and liberators.

children.name: Blessing of the Trickster
children.description: You grant Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to yourself or a willing creature. The effect lasts until you finish a Long Rest or use this feature again.

children.name: Invoke Duplicity
children.description:
You create a visual illusion of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The illusion is animated and mimics your expressions and gestures. While it persists, you gain the following benefits:

**Cast Spells.** You can cast spells as though you were in the illusion’s space, but you must use your own senses.

**Distract.** When you$(meta.level >= 17 ? ' or your allies' : '') and the illusion are within 5 feet of a creature, attack rolls against it have Advantage.

**Move.** As a Bonus Action, move the illusion up to 30 feet to a space within 120 feet of you.$(meta.level >= 6 ? ' You can teleport to swap places with it.' : '')

name: War Domain
description:
War has many manifestations. It can make heroes of ordinary people. It can be desperate and horrific, with acts of cruelty and cowardice eclipsing instances of excellence and courage. Clerics who tap into the magic of the War Domain excel in battle, inspiring others to fight the good fight or offering acts of violence as prayers.

Gods of the War Domain watch over warriors and reward them for their great deeds. They include champions of honor and chivalry as well as gods of destruction and pillage. Other war gods take a more neutral stance, promoting war in all its manifestations and supporting warriors in any circumstance.

children.name: Guided Strike
children.description: When you or a creature within range misses with an attack roll, you give that roll a +10 bonus, potentially causing it to hit.

children.name: War Priest
children.description: You make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

name: Circle of the Land
description: The Circle of the Land comprises mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites. These Druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle’s wisest members preside as the chief priests of their communities.

children.name: Land's Aid
children.description: Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear in a point you choose within range. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw, taking $(meta.level >= 14 ? '4d6' : meta.level >= 10 ? '3d6' : '2d6') Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. One creature of your choice in that area regains $(meta.level >= 14 ? '4d6' : meta.level >= 10 ? '3d6' : '2d6') Hit Points.

name: Circle of the Moon
description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon draw on lunar magic to transform themselves. Their order gathers under the moon to share news and perform rituals. Changeable as the moon, a Druid of this circle might prowl as a great cat one night, soar over the treetops as an eagle the next day, and then crash through undergrowth as a bear to drive off a trespassing monster.

children.name: Lunar Radiance
children.description: You can deal Radiant damage instead of the normal type.

children.name: Increased Toughness
children.description: You can add $(stats.wis.mod) to your Constitution saving throws.

value.name: Multiattack
value.description: The beast makes two Bestial Strike attacks.

name: Circle of the Sea
description: Druids of the Circle of the Sea draw on the tempestuous forces of oceans and storms. Some view themselves as embodiments of nature's wrath, seeking vengeance against those who despoil nature. Others seek mystical unity with nature by attuning themselves to the ebb and flow of the tides.

children.name: Wrath of the Sea
children.description:
You manifest a $(meta.level >= 6 ? 10 : 5)-foot Emanation of ocean spray around you$(meta.level >= 14 ? " or a willing creature within 60 feet. You can manifest it around both the other creature and yourself if you expend two uses of your Wild Shape instead of one" : "").

When you manifest it, and as a Bonus Action on your subsequent turns, you can choose a creature you can see in the Emanation. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw or take $(Math.max(1, stats.wis.mod))d6 Cold damage$(meta.level >= 6 ? " and be pushed up to 15 feet away (if Large or smaller)" : "").

name: Circle of the Stars
description: The Circle of the Stars has tracked heavenly patterns since time immemorial, discovering secrets hidden amid the constellations. By understanding these secrets, the Druids of this circle seek to harness the powers of the cosmos.

children.name: Starry Form
children.description:
You take on a starry form. While in your starry form, you retain your game statistics, but your body becomes luminous, your joints glimmer like stars, and glowing lines connect them as on a star chart. This form sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. $(meta.level >= 14 ? "Additionally, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage." : "")

Whenever you assume your starry form, choose which of the following constellations glimmers on your body; your choice gives you certain benefits while in the form.

**Archer.** You hurl a mote of light at a creature or object within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 10 ? 2 : 1)d8$(formatBonus(stats.wis.mod)) Radiant damage. You can repeat this attack as a Bonus Action while the form lasts.

**Chalice.** When you cast a healing spell with a slot, you or creature within 30 feet regains $(meta.level >= 10 ? 2 : 1)d8$(formatBonus(stats.wis.mod)) HP.

**Dragon.** When you make an Intelligence or a Wisdom check or a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. $(meta.level >= 10 ? "You have a Fly Speed of 20 feet and can hover." : "")

children.name: Cosmic Omen
children.description:
When you finish a Long Rest, you consult your Star Map for omens and roll a die. Until you finish your next Long Rest, you gain access to a special Reaction based on whether you rolled an even or an odd number on the die:

**Weal (Even).** When a creature is about to make a D20 Test, roll 1d6 and add it to the total.

**Woe (Odd).** When a creature is about to make a D20 Test, roll 1d6 and subtract it from the total.

name: Battle Master
description: Battle Masters are students of the art of battle, learning martial techniques passed down through generations. The most accomplished Battle Masters are well-rounded figures who combine their carefully honed combat skills with academic study in the fields of history, theory, and the arts.

children.name: Student of War Skill Proficiency
children.target: acrobaticsProficiency OR animalHandlingProficiency OR athleticsProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR persuasionProficiency OR perceptionProficiency OR survivalProficiency

children.name: Student of War Tool Proficiency
children.target: tool NOT gamingSet NOT musicalInstrument NOT thievesTools NOT poisonersKit NOT navigatorsTools NOT herbalismKit NOT forgeryKit NOT disguiseKit OR artisansToolsProficiency

children.name: Know Your Enemy
children.description: You discern certain strengths and weaknesses of a creature you can see within range. You know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.

name: Champion
description: A Champion focuses on the development of martial prowess in a relentless pursuit of victory. Champions combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows, withstand peril, and garner glory. Whether in athletic contests or bloody battle, Champions strive for the crown of the victor.

children.name: Improved Critical
children.description: You score a Critical Hit on a roll of $(meta.level >= 15 ? '18-20' : '19 or 20').

children.name: Remarkable Athlete
children.description: On a Critical Hit, you can move up to $(attributes.movement.walk/2) feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

name: Eldritch Knight
description: Eldritch Knights combine the martial mastery common to all Fighters with a careful study of magic. Their spells both complement and extend their combat skills, providing additional protection to shore up their armor and also allowing them to engage many foes at once with explosive magic.

children.name: Eldritch Knight Spells
children.target: wizard NOT class NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

children.name: War Bond
children.description: You summon your bonded weapon, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand. You can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

children.target: activities[tags=weaponAttack].extra["instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn."]
children.value: instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. You can replace one of the attacks with a cantrip.

name: Psi Warrior
description: Psi Warriors awaken the power of their minds to augment their physical might. They harness this psionic power to infuse their weapon strikes, lash out with telekinetic energy, and create barriers of mental force.

children.name: Protective Field
children.description: When you or another creature you can see within range takes damage, you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force. Reduce the damage taken by 1d$(attributes.psiDice)$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)).

children.name: Psionic Strike
children.description: When you hit a target with a weapon attack, you propel your weapon with psionic force. The target takes 1d$(attributes.psiDice)$(formatBonus(stats.int.mod)) Force damage.

children.name: Telekinetic Movementlimite
children.description: You move an object or creature with your mind. Choose one target you can see within range; the target must be a loose object that is Large or smaller, or one willing creature other than you. You transport the target up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, if the target is a Tiny object, you can transport it to or from your hand.

children.name: Psi Powered Leap
children.description: You gain a Fly Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk * 2) feet until the end of the current turn.

children.name: Telekinetic Thrust
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(8+ attributes.prof + stats.int.mod) Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you can give the target the Prone condition or transport it up to 10 feet horizontally.

name: Warrior of the Elements
description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Harnessing their supernatural focus, these Monks momentarily tame the energy of the Elemental Chaos to empower themselves in and out of battle.

children.name: Elemental Attunement
children.description:
You imbue yourself with elemental energy. While active, gain the following benefits:

**Reach** Your Unarmed Strike reach is 5 feet greater than normal, as elemental energy extends from you.

**Elemental Strikes** You can cause Unarmed Strikes to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage. When you deal one of these types, force the target to make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Strength saving throw. On a failure, move the target up to 5 feet toward or away from you.

children.name: Elemental Burst
children.description: You cause elemental energy to burst in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Each creature in the Sphere must make a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d$(attributes.martialArtsDie) damage of the chosen type on a failure or half as much on a success.

name: Warrior of Mercy
description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians, but they bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.

children.name: Hand of Harm
children.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you deal 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) extra Necrotic damage.$(meta.level >= 6 ? ' You can also give the target the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.' : '')

children.name: Hand of Healing
children.description: You touch a creature and restore 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) Hit Points to it.$(meta.level >= 6 ? ' You can also end one condition on the creature: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned.' : '')

name: Warrior of the Open Hand
description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and manipulate their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

children.name: Open Hand Technique
children.description: When you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you impose one of the following effects on the target.

children.name: Addle
children.description: The target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

children.name: Push
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.

children.name: Topple
children.description: The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.monkSaveDC) Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition.

children.name: Wholeness of Body
children.description: You channel your inner healing energy. Regain 1d$(attributes.martialArtsDie)+$(stats.wis.mod) Hit Points.

name: Warrior of Shadow
description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, harnessing the power of the Shadowfell. They are at home in darkness, able to draw gloom around themselves to hide, leap from shadow to shadow, and take on a wraithlike form.

children.name: Shadow Step
children.description: While entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, you teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. You then have Advantage on the next melee attack you make before the end of the current turn.

name: Oath of Devotion
description: The Oath of Devotion binds Paladins to the ideals of Justice and Order. These Paladins meet the archetype of the knight in shining armor.

children.name: Sacred Weapon
children.description:
When you take the Attack action, you imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For the duration or until you use this feature again, you add $(formatBonus(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod))) to attack rolls you make with that weapon, and each time you hit with it, you cause it to deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. 

The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light 20 feet beyond that. You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.

children.name: Aura of Devotion
children.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the Charmed condition. If a Charmed ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there.

name: Oath of Glory
description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism.

children.name: Inspiring Smite
children.description: Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you distribute Temporary Hit Points to creatures of your choice within range, which can include you. The total number of Temporary Hit Points equals 2d8$(formatBonus(meta.level)), divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

children.name: Peerless Athlete
children.description: You augment your athleticism. For the duration, you have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, and the distance of your Long and High Jumps increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).

children.name: Aura of Alacrity
children.description: Whenever an ally enters the aura for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there, the ally's Speed increases by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.

name: Oath of the Ancients
description: Paladins who swear this oath cherish the light; they love the beautiful and life-giving things of the world more than any principles of honor, courage, and justice.

children.name: Nature's Wrath
children.description: You conjure spectral vines around nearby creatures. Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition for 1 minute. A Restrained creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

children.name: Aura of Warding
children.description: Ancient magic lies so heavily upon you that it forms an eldritch ward, blunting energy from beyond the Material Plane. You and your allies have Resistance to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

name: Oath of Vengeance
description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed grievously evil acts.

children.name: Vow of Enmity
children.description: When you take the Attack action, you utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within range. You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature for the duration or until you use this feature again. If the creature drops to 0 Hit Points before the vow ends, you can transfer the vow to a different creature within 30 feet of yourself (no action required).

children.name: Relentless Avenger
children.description: On a hit, you can reduce the creature's Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn, then move up to half your Speed as part of the same Reaction without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

name: Beast Master
description: A Beast Master forms a mystical bond with a special animal, drawing on primal magic and a deep connection to the natural world.

children.value: $(meta.beastcompanionname ? meta.beastcompanionname + ' (Beast of the Land)' : 'Beast of the Land')
$(meta.beastcompanionname ? meta.beastcompanionname + ' (Beast of the Sea)' : 'Beast of the Sea')
$(meta.beastcompanionname ? meta.beastcompanionname + ' (Beast of the Sky)' : 'Beast of the Sky')

children.name: Command Companion
children.description: You can replace one attack with a command to your Primal Companion beast to take the Beast's Strike action$(meta.level >= 11 ? ' twice' : '').

children.name: Command Companion
children.description: You command your Primal Companion beast to take an action. The beast acts during your turn and can move and use its Reaction on its own, but only takes the Dodge action unless you command it.

children.name: Exceptional Training
children.description: You can also command the beast to take the Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action using its Bonus Action. Whenever it hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

name: Fey Wanderer
description: A fey mystique surrounds you, thanks to the boon of an archfey or a location in the Feywild that transformed you. Your joyful laughter brightens the hearts of the downtrodden, and your martial prowess strikes terror in your foes, for great is the mirth of the fey and dreadful is their fury.

children.name: Dreadful Strikes
children.description: On a hit, once per turn, deal an extra $(meta.level >= 11 ? '1d6' : '1d4') Psychic damage to the target.

children.name: Beguiling Twist
children.description: When you or a creature you can see within 120 feet succeeds on a saving throw to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition, you force a different creature you can see within 120 feet to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom save. On a failure, the target is Charmed or Frightened (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

name: Gloom Stalker
description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding magic drawn from the Shadowfell to combat enemies that lurk in darkness.

children.name: Ambusher's Leap
children.description: At the start of your first turn of each combat, your speed increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.

children.name: Dreadful Strike
children.description: Once per turn when you attack a creature and hit it with a weapon, you deal an extra $(meta.level >= 11 ? '2d8' : '2d6') Psychic damage. $(meta.level >= 11 ? 'Additionally, you can cause one of the following effects:' : '')

children.name: Umbral Sight
