children.description: While entirely in Darkness, you have the Invisible condition to any creature that relies on Darkvision to see you in that Darkness.

name: Hunter
description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people everywhere from forces that would destroy them.

children.name: Hunter's Lore
children.description: You know whether the creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if it has any, you know what they are.

name: Arcane Trickster
description: Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with spells, learning magical tricks to aid them in their trade. Some Arcane Tricksters use their talents as pickpockets and burglars, while others are pranksters.

children.name: Arcane Trickster Spells
children.target: wizard NOT class NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

name: Assassin
description: An Assassin's training focuses on using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency. While some Rogues who follow this path are hired killers, spies, or bounty hunters, the capabilities of this subclass are equally useful for adventurers facing a variety of monstrous enemies.

children.name: Surprising Strike
children.description: On a hit during the first round of combat, the target takes $(meta.level) extra damage.

children.name: Surprising Strike
children.description: During the first round of each combat, you have Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn.

name: Soulknife
description: A Soulknife strikes with the mind, cutting through barriers both physical and psychic. These Rogues discover psionic power within themselves and channel it to do their roguish work. As a Soulknife your psionic abilities might have haunted you since childhood, revealing their full potential only as you experienced the stress of adventure. Or you might have sought out an order of psychic adepts and spent years learning how to manifest your power.

children.name: Psi-Bolstered Knack
children.description: When you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you add 1d$(attributes.psiDice) to the check, potentially turning failure into success. The die is expended only if the roll then succeeds.

children.name: Psychic Whispers
children.description: You choose up to $(attributes.prof) creatures you can see. For 1d$(attributes.psiDice) hours, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically to you, and you can speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other. A creature can end the telepathic connection at any time (no action required).

children.name: Psychic Blade
children.description:
You manifest a shimmering blade of psychic energy and strike at a target within range. Make a $(formatBonus(stats.dex.mod + attributes.prof)) attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d6$(formatBonus(stats.dex.mod)) Psychic damage. The blade vanishes immediately after it hits or misses, leaving no mark.

After you attack with the blade on your turn, you can make a melee or ranged attack with a second psychic blade as a Bonus Action on the same turn. The damage die of this bonus attack is 1d4 instead of 1d6.

name: Thief
description: A mix of burglar, treasure hunter, and explorer, you are the epitome of an adventurer. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you gain abilities useful for delving into ruins and getting maximum benefit from the magic items you find there.

children.name: Fast Hands
children.description:
You do one of the following.

**Sleight of Hand.** Make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with Thieves' Tools or to pick a pocket.

**Use an Object.** Take the Utilize action, or take the Magic action to use a magic item that requires an action.

name: Aberrant Sorcery
description: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you. Will this power shine from you as a hopeful beacon to others? Or will you be a terror to those who feel the stab of your mind?

name: Clockwork Sorcery
description: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency. You or someone from your lineage might have become entangled in the machinations of modrons, the orderly beings who inhabit Mechanus.

children.name: Restore Balance
children.description: When a creature you can see rolls with Advantage or Disadvantage, you prevent the roll from being affected by it.

children.name: Bastion of Law
children.description:
You tap into the grand equation of existence to imbue a creature with a shimmering shield of order. Create a magical ward around a creature within range. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of Sorcery Points spent to create it. When the warded creature takes damage, it can expend a number of those dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled on those dice.

The ward lasts until you finish a Long Rest or until you use this feature again.

name: Draconic Sorcery
description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with dragons’ power. Or perhaps you handled a treasure taken from a dragon’s hoard that was steeped in draconic power.

name: Wild Magic Sorcery
description: Your innate magic stems from the forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. You or an ancestor might have endured exposure to raw magic, perhaps through a planar portal leading to Limbo or the Elemental Planes. Perhaps you were blessed by a fey being or marked by a demon. Or your magic could be a fluke with no apparent cause. Whatever its source, this magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

children.name: Wild Magic Surge
children.description: Your spellcasting unleashes surges of untamed magic. Once per turn, roll 1d20 immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.

children.name: Tides of Chaos
children.description:
You manipulate chaos itself to give yourself Advantage on one D20 Test before you roll.
Once you do so, you must cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot or finish a Long Rest before you can use this feature again.

If you do cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot before you finish a Long Rest, you automatically roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.

children.name: Bend Luck
children.description: You twist fate using your wild magic. Immediately after another creature you can see rolls the d20 for a D20 Test, roll 1d4 and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the d20 roll.

name: Archfey Patron
description: Your pact draws on the power of the Feywild. When you choose this subclass, you might make a deal with an archfey, such as the Prince of Frost; the Queen of Air and Darkness, ruler of the Gloaming Court; Titania of the Summer Court; or an ancient hag.

overwrite.extra:
In addition, you can choose one of the following additional effects:

**Refreshing Step.** You or a creature within 10 feet of yourself gains 1d10 Temporary Hit Points.

**Taunting Step.** Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

$(meta.level >= 6 ? "**Disappearing Step.** You have the invisible condition until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you attack or cast a spell." : "")

$(meta.level >= 6 ? "**Dreadful Step.** Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left or the space you appear in must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or take 2d10 Psychic damage." : "")

children.name: Misty Escape
children.description: When you take damage, you cast Misty Step.

name: Celestial Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Upper Planes, the realms of everlasting bliss. You might enter an agreement with an empyrean, a couatl, a sphinx, a unicorn, or another heavenly entity. Or you might call on numerous such beings as you pursue goals aligned with theirs.

children.name: Healing Light
children.description: Heal yourself or a creature you can see. The target regains 1d6 Hit Points per charge expeded, up to a maximum of $(Math.min(1, stats.cha.mod))d6 Hit Points.

name: Fiend Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, the realms of perdition. You might forge a bargain with a demon lord such as Demogorgon or Orcus; an archdevil such as Asmodeus; or a pit fiend.

children.name: Dark One's Blessing
children.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain $(meta.level + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points. You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.

children.name: Dark One's Own Luck
children.description: You call on your fiendish patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add 1d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the roll but before any of the roll’s effects occur.

children.name: Fiendish Resilience
children.description: Choose one damage type (other than Force). You have Resistance to it.

children.name: Hurl Through Hell
children.description:
Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you try to instantly transport the target through the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Charisma saving throw, or the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. 

The target takes 8d10 Psychic damage if it isn’t a Fiend, and it has the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn, when it returns to the space it previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space.

name: Great Old One Patron
description: Your pact draws on the Far Realm or an elder god—a being such as Tharizdun, Hadar, or Great Cthulhu. The motives of these beings are incomprehensible, and the Great Old One might be indifferent to your existence.

children.name: Awakened Mind
children.description: You form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a Bonus Action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within $(Math.max(1, stats.cha.mod)) miles of each other. To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows.

children.target: warlock AND damageSpell NOT (enchantment OR illusion)
children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can change the damage type to Psychic.

children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal or Somatic components.

children.target: warlock AND (enchantment OR illusion) AND damageSpell
children.name: Psychic Spells
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal or Somatic components and change the damage type to Psychic.

children.name: Clairvoyant Combatant
children.description: When you form a telepathic bond with a creature using your Awakened Mind, you force that creature to make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, and you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature for the duration of the bond.

name: Abjurer
description: Your study of magic is focused on spells that block, banish, or protect—ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and protecting the weak. Abjurers are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to other planes of existence must be closed.

children.name: Arcane Ward
children.description:
When you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, you weave magic around yourself for protection. You simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a ward with $(2 * meta.level + stats.int.mod) Hit Points that lasts until you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead, and if you have any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward’s Hit Points. If the damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it can’t absorb damage, but its magic remains. 

Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot. Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can expend a spell slot, and the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot expended.

children.name: Projected Ward
children.description: When a creature that you can see within range takes damage, you cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage. If that creature has any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward's Hit Points.

name: Diviner
description: The counsel of a Diviner is sought by those who want a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

children.name: Portent
children.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, glimpses on the future press on your awareness. Roll $(meta.level >= 14 ? '3' : '2')d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any D20 Test made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

children.name: Expert Divination
children.description: You regain an expended spell slot. The spell slot can be of a level lower than the slot you used, but no higher than 5th level.

name: Evoker
description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some Evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast armies from afar. Others use their power to protect others, while some seek their own gain.

children.name: Potent Cantrip
children.description: If the target succeeds on a saving throw against this cantrip, it takes half damage but suffers no additional effect.

children.name: Sculpt Spells
children.description: When you cast this spell, your allies automatically succeed on their saving throw and take no damage if they would normally take half.

name: Illusionist
description: You specialize in magic that dazzles the senses and tricks the mind, and the illusions you craft make the impossible seem real. Most Wizards believe that their counterparts in ancient civilizations knew secrets of magic that have been lost to the ages, and discovering those secrets could unlock the path to a power greater than any magic available in the present age.

children.target: activities[tags=illusion AND NOT range=self AND NOT range=touch].range

children.name: Improved Illusions
children.description: You can cast this spell without Verbal components.

name: Warlock
description: Warlocks quest for knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse. They often begin their search for magical power by delving into tomes of forbidden lore, dabbling in invocations meant to attract the power of extraplanar beings, or seeking places of power where the influence of these beings can be felt.

children.name: Warlock Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR deceptionProficiency OR historyProficiency OR intimidationProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR natureProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Warlock Prepared Spells
children.target: warlock NOT level9Spell NOT level8Spell NOT level7Spell NOT level6Spell NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

name: Wizard
description: Wizards are defined by their exhaustive study of magic's inner workings. They cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their magic conjures monsters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future, or forms protective barriers. Their mightiest spells change one substance into another, call meteors from the sky, or open portals to other worlds.

children.name: Wizard Skill Proficiencies
children.target: arcanaProficiency OR historyProficiency OR insightProficiency OR investigationProficiency OR medicineProficiency OR natureProficiency OR religionProficiency

children.name: Wizard Prepared Spells
children.target: wizard NOT cantrip $(local.maxLevel < 9 ? 'NOT level9Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 8 ? 'NOT level8Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 7 ? 'NOT level7Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 6 ? 'NOT level6Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 5 ? 'NOT level5Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 4 ? 'NOT level4Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 3 ? 'NOT level3Spell' : '') $(local.maxLevel < 2 ? 'NOT level2Spell' : '')

variables.maxLevel: $(meta.level >= 17 ? 9 : meta.level >= 15 ? 8 : meta.level >= 13 ? 7 : meta.level >= 11 ? 6 : meta.level >= 9 ? 5 : meta.level >= 7 ? 4 : meta.level >= 5 ? 3 : meta.level >= 3 ? 2 : 1)

children.name: Scholar Skill Expertise
children.target: arcanaExpertise OR historyExpertise OR insightExpertise OR investigationExpertise OR medicineExpertise OR natureExpertise OR religionExpertise

children.name: Dash
children.description: You gain extra movement for the current turn. Your Speed increases by $(attributes.movement.walk).

children.name: Disengage
children.description: Your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks for the rest of the current turn.

children.name: Hide
children.description: You try to conceal yourself while you're Heavily Obscured or behind Three-Quarters Cover or Total Cover. Make a $(formatBonus(skills.stealth.bonus)) DC 15 Dexterity (Stealth) check. On a succesful check, you have the Invisible condition until you make a sound louder than a whisper, an enemy finds you, you make an attack roll, or you cast a spell with a Verbal component.

children.name: Dodge
children.description: Any attack roll made against you has Disadvantage if you can see the attacker, and you make Dexterity saving throws with Advantage.

children.name: Study
children.description:
You make an Intelligence check to study your memory, a book, a clue, or another source of knowledge and call to mind an important piece of information about it.

**Arcana ($(formatBonus(skills.arcana.bonus))).** Spells, magic items, eldritch symbols, magical traditions, planes of existence, and certain creatures (Aberrations, Constructs, Elementals, Fey, and Monstrosities)

**History ($(formatBonus(skills.history.bonus))).** Historic events and people, ancient civilizations, wars, and certain creatures (Giants and Humanoids)

**Investigation ($(formatBonus(skills.investigation.bonus))).** Traps, ciphers, riddles, and gadgetry

**Nature ($(formatBonus(skills.nature.bonus))).** Terrain, flora, weather, and certain creatures (Beasts, Dragons, Oozes, and Plants)

**Religion ($(formatBonus(skills.religion.bonus))).** Deities, religious hierarchies and rites, holy symbols, cults, and certain creatures (Celestials, Fiends, and Undead)

children.name: Search
children.description:
You make a Wisdom check to discern something that isn't obvious. 

**Insight ($(formatBonus(skills.insight.bonus))).** Creature's state of mind

**Medicine ($(formatBonus(skills.medicine.bonus))).** Creature's ailment or cause of death

**Perception ($(formatBonus(skills.perception.bonus))).** Concealed creature or object

**Survival ($(formatBonus(skills.survival.bonus))).** Tracks or food

children.name: Influence
children.description:
You make a Wisdom or Charisma check to urge a creature or group of creatures to do something. 

**Deception ($(formatBonus(skills.deception.bonus))).** Lie

**Intimidation ($(formatBonus(skills.intimidation.bonus))).** Threaten

**Performance ($(formatBonus(skills.performance.bonus))).** Entertain

**Persuasion ($(formatBonus(skills.persuasion.bonus))).** Persuade

**Animal Handling ($(formatBonus(skills.animal_handling.bonus))).** Control or train a Beast

children.name: Help
children.description:
You do one of the following.

**Assist an Ability Check.** Choose one of your skill or tool proficiencies and one ally who is near enough for you to assist verbally or physically when they make an ability check. That ally has Advantage on the next ability check they make with the chosen skill or tool. This benefit expires if the ally doesn't use it before the start of your next turn. The DM has final say on whether your assistance is possible.

**Assist an Attack Roll.** You momentarily distract an enemy within 5 feet of you, giving Advantage to the next attack roll by one of your allies against that enemy. This benefit expires at the start of your next turn.

children.name: Ready
children.description:
You take the Ready action to wait for a particular circumstance before you act. To do so, you take this action on your turn, which lets you act by taking a Reaction before the start of your next turn.

First, you decide what perceivable circumstance will trigger your Reaction. Then, you choose the action you will take in response to that trigger, or you choose to move up to your Speed in response to it. Examples include "If the cultist steps on the trapdoor, I'll pull the lever that opens it," and "If the zombie steps next to me, I move away."

When the trigger occurs, you can either take your Reaction right after the trigger finishes or ignore the trigger.

name: Long Rest
description:
A Long Rest is a period of extended downtime—at least 8 hours—available to any creature. During a Long Rest, you sleep for at least 6 hours and perform no more than 2 hours of light activity, such as reading, talking, eating, or standing watch.
During sleep, you have the Unconscious condition. After you finish a Long Rest, you must wait at least 16 hours before starting another one.
You regain all lost Hit Points and all spent Hit Point Dice.

name: Opportunity Attack
description: When a creature leaves your reach, you make one melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against the target. The attack occurs right before the creature leaves your reach.

name: Short Rest
description:
A Short Rest is a 1-hour period of downtime, during which a creature does nothing more strenuous than reading, talking, eating, or standing watch. To start a Short Rest, you must have at least 1 Hit Point.

**Spend Hit Point Dice.** You can spend one or more of your Hit Point Dice to regain Hit Points. For each Hit Point Die you spend in this way, you regain 1d$(attributes.hitDie)$(formatBonus(stats.con.mod)) Hit Points. You can decide to spend an additional Hit Point Die after each roll.

name: $(local.name)
description: You strike with your weapon. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes[local.classification].attack)) attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(local.damageRoll)$(formatBonus(attributes[local.classification].bonus)) $(capitalize(local.damageType)) damage.

name: Unarmed Strike
description: You strike with a punch, kick, head-butt or similar forceful blow. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.melee.attack)) attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d4$(formatBonus(attributes.melee.bonus)) Bludgeoning damage.

children.name: Ritual
children.description: You can cast this spell as a Ritual instead. The Ritual version of a spell takes 10 minutes longer to cast, and doesn't expend a Spell Slot.

children.name: Light
children.description: You can make an additional attack with a Light weapon as a Bonus Action. You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll.

children.name: Grapple or Shove
children.description: You can replace one attack with a grapple or shove attempt. The target must succeed on a DC $(8+attributes.prof+Math.max(stats.str.mod, stats.dex.mod)) Dexterity saving throw or it has the Grappled or Prone condition.

children.name: Graze
children.description: On a miss, deal $(attributes[local.classification].bonus) $(local.damageType) damage.

children.name: Slow
children.description: On a hit, the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet.

children.name: Nick
children.description: You can make the additional attack of the Light property as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action.

children.name: Cleave
children.description: On a hit, you can make another attack against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach.

children.name: Push
children.description: On a hit, push the target up to 10 feet straight away from yourself.

children.name: Topple
children.description: On a hit, the target must succeed a DC $(8 + attributes.prof + attributes[local.classification].bonus) Constitution saving throw or have the Prone condition.

children.name: Sap
children.description: On a hit, the target has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

children.name: Vex
children.description: On a hit, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against the target before the end of your next turn.

name: Great Weapon Fighting
description: On a hit, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3.

name: Interception
description: When a creature you can see hits another creature with an attack roll, you reduce the damage dealt to the target by 1d10+$(attributes.prof).

name: Protection
description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you, you impose Disadvantage on the triggering attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn.

target: activities[tags=weaponAttack].extra["You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll."]
name: Two Weapon Fighting

children.name: Unarmed Fighting
children.description: At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

children.name: Improved Dash
children.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet for that action.

children.name: Charge
children.description: If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line toward the target immediately before hitting it, choose one of the following effects — gain a 1d8 bonus to the attack's damage roll, or push the target up to 10 feet away if it is no more than one size larger than you.

children.name: Replenishing Meal
children.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can cook special food if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for $(4 + attributes.prof) creatures. Any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain Hit Points regains an extra 1d8 Hit Points.

children.name: Bolstering Treats
children.description: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a Long Rest, you cook a number of treats equal to $(attributes.prof) if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a Bonus Action to eat one of those treats to gain $(attributes.prof) Temporary Hit Points.

children.target: activities[tags=weaponAttack].extra["You do not add your Ability Modifier to the attack's damage roll"].description

children.name: Push
children.description: On a hit, you can move the target 5 feet to an unoccupied space if the target is no more than one size larger than you.

children.name: Enhanced Critical
children.description: On a Critical Hit, attack rolls against the target have Advantage until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Parry
children.description: When you are hit with an attack, you add $(attributes.prof) to your Armor Class, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You gain this bonus to your AC against melee attacks until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Speedy Recovery
children.description: You can expend one of your Hit Dice, roll the die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll.

children.name: Energy Mastery
children.target: acidResistance OR coldResistance OR fireResistance OR lightningResistance or thunderResistance OR elementalAdeptFeatResistance

children.name: Attack Advantage
children.description: You have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature Grappled by you.

children.name: Fast Wrestler
children.description: You don't have to spend extra movement to move a creature Grappled by you if the creature is your size or smaller.

children.name: Grapple
children.description: When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use both the Damage and the Grapple option. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

children.name: Hew
children.description: On a Critical Hit, or when you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points, make another attack.

children.name: Damage Reduction
children.description: When you're hit by an attack, any Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage dealt to you by that attack is reduced by $(attributes.prof).

children.name: Bolstering Performance
children.duration: until next short or long rest
children.description: At the end of a Short Rest or Long Rest, you can give an inspiring performance — a speech, song, or dance. Choose up to $(5 + attributes.prof) allies who witnessed the performance. The chosen creatures each gain $(meta.level + stats.cha.mod) Temporary Hit Points.

children.name: Lore Knowledge Skill Proficiency
children.target: arcanaProficiency or historyProficiency or investigationProficiency or natureProficiency or religionProficiency

children.name: Concentration Breaker
children.description: On a hit, the target has Disadvantage on any saving throw it makes to maintain Concentration.

children.name: Guarded Mind
children.description: If you fail an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead.

children.name: Mounted Combatant
children.description:
While mounted, you have Advantage on attack rolls against any unmounted creature within 5 feet of your mount that is at least one size smaller than the mount.

If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw and only half damage if it fails. For your mount to gain this benefit, you must be riding it, and neither of you can have the Incapacitated condition.

While you're riding a mount that isn't Incapacitated, you can force an attack that hits the mount to hit you instead if you don't have the Incapacitated condition.

children.name: Puncture
children.description: On a hit, you can reroll one of the attack's damage dice, and you must use the new roll.

children.name: Enhanced Critical
children.description: On a Critical Hit, you can roll one additional damage die.

children.name: Potent Poison
children.description: This damage ignores resistance to Poison damage.

children.name: Brew Poison
children.description: You can craft poisons. With 1 hour of work using a Poisoner's Kit and expending 50 GP worth of materials, you can create $(attributes.prof) doses of poison. As a Bonus Action, you can apply a poison dose to a weapon or piece of ammunition. Once applied, the poison retains its potency for 1 minute or until you hit with the poisoned item, whichever comes first. When a creature takes damage from the poisoned item, that creature must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.dex.mod + attributes.prof) Constitution saving throw or take 2d8 Poison damage and have the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

children.name: Reactive Strike
children.description: You can make an Opportunity Attack when a creature enters your reach.

children.name: Pole Strike
children.description: When you make an attack with a Quarterstaff, Spear, Glaive, Halberd, Lance, or Pike, make a $(formatBonus(attributes.melee.bonus)) attack with the opposite end of it. On a hit, deal 1d4$(formatBonus(attributes.melee.bonus)) Bludgeoning damage.

children.name: Saving Throw Proficiency
target: strSave or dexSave or conSave or intSave or wisSave or chaSave

children.name: Quick Ritual
children.description: You can cast this spell using its regular casting time rather than the extended time for a Ritual. Doing so doesn't require a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

children.name: Halt
children.description: On a hit, the target's Speed becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

children.name: Guardian
children.description: You can make an Opportunity Attack when a creature takes the Disengage action or hits a target other than you with an attack.

children.name: Bypass Cover
children.description: This attack ignores Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover.

children.name: Firing in Melee
children.description: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on this attack.

children.name: Precision Shot
children.description: On a hit, the target takes $(attributes.prof) extra damage. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

children.name: Shield Bash
children.description: If you attack a creature on your turn, you try to shove that creature with your Shield. The target must succeed on a DC $(8+attributes.prof+stats.str.mod) Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

children.name: Interpose Shield
children.description: If you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw.

children.name: Sniper
children.description: On a miss, while hidden, making the attack roll doesn't reveal your location.

children.name: Hamstring
children.description: On a hit, the target's Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Enhanced Critical
children.description: On a Critical Hit, the target has Disadvantage on all attack rolls until the start of the next turn.

children.name: Agile Movement
children.description: Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.

children.name: Bypass Cover
children.description: This attack ignores Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover.

children.name: Casting in Melee
children.description: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on this attack.

children.name: Telekinetic Shove
children.description: You try to telekinetically shove one creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(8 + stats.int.mod + attributes.prof) Strength saving throw or be moved 5 feet toward or away from you. A creature can willingly fail this save.

children.name: Reactive Spell
children.description: You can cast a spell rather than making an Opportunity Attack. The spell must have a casting time of one action and must target only that creature.

children.name: Initiative Swap
children.description: When you roll Initiative, you swap your Initiative with the Initiative of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the Incapacitated condition.

children.name: Fast Crafting
Battle Medic
children.description: You tend to a creature within range. That creature can expend one of its Hit Point Dice, and you then roll that die. The creature regains Hit Points equal to the roll plus a $(attributes.prof) Bonus.

children.name: Healing Rerolls
children.description: You can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.

children.name: Lucky
children.description: When you roll a d20 for a D20 Test, or a creature rolls a d20 for an attack roll against you, give yourself Advantage on the roll, or impose Disadvantage on the roll, respectively.

children.name: Encouraging Song
children.description: When you finish a Short or Long Rest, you play a song on a Musical Instrument with which you have proficiency and give Heroic Inspiration to $(attributes.prof) allies who hear the song.

name: Savage Attacker
description: On a hit, roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

children.name: Damage Rerolls
children.description: You can reroll the damage die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.

children.name: Push
children.description: On a hit, you can push the target 5 feet away from you.

name: Aasimar
description: Aasimar (pronounced AH-sih-mar) are mortals who carry a spark of the Upper Planes within their souls. Whether descended from an angelic being or infused with celestial power, they can fan that spark to bring light, healing, and heavenly fury. Aasimar can arise among any population of mortals. They resemble their parents, but they live for up to 160 years and have features that hint at their celestial heritage.

children.name: Healing Hands
children.description: You touch a creature and roll $(attributes.prof)d4. The creature regains Hit Points equal to the total rolled.

children.name: Celestial Revelation
children.description:
You transform. The transformation lasts for the duration or until you end it (no action required). Once on each of your turns before it ends, you can deal $(attributes.prof) extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell; the damage is Necrotic for Necrotic Shroud or Radiant for Heavenly Wings and Inner Radiance.

**Heavenly Wings.** You have a Fly Speed of $(attributes.movement.walk) until the transformation ends.

**Inner Radiance.** You shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. At the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes $(attributes.prof) Radiant damage.

**Necrotic Shroud.** Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a DC $(attributes.prof + 8 + stats.cha.mod) Charisma saving throw or have the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

name: Dragonborn
description: The ancestors of dragonborn hatched from the eggs of chromatic and metallic dragons. Dragonborn look like wingless, bipedal dragons—scaly, bright-eyed, and thick-boned with horns on their heads—and their coloration and other features are reminiscent of their draconic ancestors.

children.name: Breath Weapon
children.description: You exhale magical energy in a 15-foot Cone or a 30-foot Line (5 feet wide). Each creature in that area must make a DC $(8 + stats.con.mod + attributes.prof) Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? '4' : meta.level >= 11 ? '3' : meta.level >= 5 ? '2' : '1')d10 $(attributes.dragonicAncestryDamage) damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

children.name: Draconic Flight
children.description: You sprout spectral wings on your back for 10 minutes or until you retract them (no action required) or have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.

name: Dwarf
description: Dwarves were raised from the earth in the elder days by a deity of the forge. That god gave dwarves an affinity for stone and metal and for living underground, and made them resilient like the mountains, with a life span of about 350 years.

children.name: Stonecunning
children.description: You gain Tremorsense with a range of 60 feet for 10 minutes. You must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this Tremorsense. The stone can be natural or worked.

name: Elf
description: Created by the god Corellon, the first elves could change their forms at will. No longer able to shape-shift at will, the elves retreated to the Feywild. Elves have pointed ears and lack facial and body hair. They live for around 750 years, and they don't sleep but instead enter a trance when they need to rest.

children.name: Trance
children.description: You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

children.name: Clockwork Device
children.description: You spend 10 minutes casting Prestidigitation to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP), such as a toy, fire starter, or music box. When you create the device, you determine its function by choosing one effect from Prestidigitation; the device produces that effect whenever you or another creature takes a Bonus Action to activate it with a touch. You can have three such devices in existence at a time, and each falls apart 8 hours after its creation or when you dismantle it with a touch as a Utilize action.

name: Gnome
description: Gnomes are magical folk created by gods of invention, illusions, and life underground. Gnomes are petite folk with big eyes and pointed ears, who live around 425 years.

children.name: Cloud's Jaunt
children.description: You magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

children.name: Fire's Burn
children.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target.

children.name: Frost's Chill
children.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you also deal 1d6 Cold damage to that target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

children.name: Hill's Tumble
children.description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you give that target the Prone condition.

children.name: Stone's Endurance
children.description: When you take damage, reduce it by 1d12$(formatBonus(stats.con.mod)).

children.name: Storm's Thunder
children.description: When you take damage from a creature within range, deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature.

name: Goliath
description: Towering over most folk, goliaths are distant descendants of giants. Each goliath bears the favors of the first giants—favors that manifest in various supernatural boons, including the ability to quickly grow and temporarily approach the height of goliaths' gigantic kin.

children.name: Large Form
children.description: You change your size to Large if you're in a big enough space. This transformation lasts for 10 minutes or until you end it (no action required). For that duration, you have Advantage on Strength checks, and your Speed increases by 10 feet.

name: Halfling
description: Cherished and guided by gods who value life, home, and hearth, halflings gravitate toward bucolic havens where family and community help shape their lives. Many halflings possess a brave and adventurous spirit. Their size helps them pass through crowds unnoticed and slip through tight spaces.

children.name: Halfling Nimbleness
children.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is a size larger than you, but you can't stop in the same space.

children.name: Luck
children.description: When you roll a 1 on the d20 of a D20 Test, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

children.name: Naturally Stealthy
children.description: You can Hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

name: Human
description: Found throughout the multiverse, humans are as varied as they are numerous, and they endeavor to achieve as much as they can in the years they are given. Their ambition and resourcefulness are commended, respected, and feared on many worlds.

children.name: Resourceful
children.description: You gain Heroic Inspiration.

name: Leonin
description: Leonin are golden-furred hunters, a proud and fiercely independent people. They value self-reliance above all else, often viewing the gods with wary skepticism as they protect their ancestral lands through martial prowess and daunting presence.

children.name: Daunting Roar
children.description: You let out a terrifying roar. Each creature of your choice within range must succeed on a DC $(attributes.prof + 8 + stats.con.mod) Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the end of your next turn.

name: Minotaur
description: Minotaurs are barrel-chested humanoids with heads resembling those of bulls. Blessed with a supernaturally strong sense of direction, minotaurs make great navigators. Some sages believe minotaurs were first created by the Lady of Pain to patrol the magical mazes that she uses to trap her foes.

name: Orc
description: Orcs trace their creation to Gruumsh, who equipped his children with gifts to help them wander great plains, vast caverns, and churning seas. Even when they turn their devotion to other gods, orcs retain Gruumsh's gifts: endurance, determination, and the ability to see in darkness.

children.name: Adrenaline Rush
children.description: You take the Dash action. When you do so, you gain $(attributes.prof) Temporary Hit Points.

children.name: Relentless Endurance
children.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead.

name: Satyr
description: Originating in the Feywild—a realm of pure emotion-satyrs thrive on the energy of merriment. They resemble elves but have goatlike legs, cloven hooves, and ram or goat horns. The magic of the fey realm has given them an innate ability to perform, to delight, and to resist magical intrusion. While they're usually found in the Feywild, satyrs do wander to other planes of existence, most often to the Material Plane. There they seek to bring a bit of their home plane's splendor to other worlds.

name: Tiefling
description: Tieflings are either born in the Lower Planes or have fiendish ancestors who originated there. A tiefling is linked by blood to a devil, a demon, or some other Fiend. This connection to the Lower Planes is the tiefling's fiendish legacy, which comes with the promise of power yet has no effect on the tiefling's moral outlook.

name: Triton
description: Originally from the Elemental Plane of Water, many tritons entered the Material Plane centuries ago in response to the growing threat of evil elementals. Those tritons spread across the worlds' oceans, protecting the surface from terrors in the deep. Over time, triton have extended their stewardship over the sea floor to the ocean's surface.

name: Acid Splash
description: You create an acidic bubble at a point within range, where it explodes in a 5-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in that Sphere must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Acid damage.

name: Blade Ward
description: Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you before the spell ends, the attacker subtracts 1d4 from the attack roll.

name: Chill Touch
description: Channeling the chill of the grave, make a melee spell attack against a target within reach. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d10 Necrotic damage, and it can’t regain Hit Points until the end of your next turn.

name: Dancing Lights
description:
You create up to four torch-size lights within range, making them appear as torches, lanterns, or glowing orbs that hover for the duration. Alternatively, you combine the four lights into one glowing Medium form that is vaguely humanlike. Whichever form you choose, each light sheds Dim Light in a 10-foot radius.

As a Bonus Action, you can move the lights up to 60 feet to a space within range. A light must be within 20 feet of another light created by this spell, and a light vanishes if it exceeds the spell’s range.

name: Druidcraft
description:
Whispering to the spirits of nature, you create one of the following effects within range.

**Weather Sensor.** You create a Tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts what the weather will be at your location for the next 24 hours. The effect might manifest as a golden orb for clear skies, a cloud for rain, falling snowflakes for snow, and so on. This effect persists for 1 round.

**Bloom.** You instantly make a flower blossom, a seed pod open, or a leaf bud bloom.

**Sensory Effect.** You create a harmless sensory effect, such as falling leaves, spectral dancing fairies, a gentle breeze, the sound of an animal, or the faint odor of skunk. The effect must fit in a 5-foot Cube.

**Fire Play.** You light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a campfire.

name: Eldritch Blast
description: You hurl $(meta.level >= 5 ? (meta.level >= 17 ? 'four' : meta.level >= 11 ? 'three' : 'two') + ' beams' : 'a beam') of crackling energy. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack $(meta.level >= 5 ? 'for each beam' : 'against one creature or object in range'). On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage.

name: Elementalism
description:
You exert control over the elements, creating one of the following effects within range.

**Beckon Air.** You create a breeze strong enough to ripple cloth, stir dust, rustle leaves, and close open doors and shutters, all in a 5-foot Cube. Doors and shutters being held open by someone or something aren’t affected.

**Beckon Earth.** You create a thin shroud of dust or sand that covers surfaces in a 5-foot-square area, or you cause a single word to appear in your handwriting in a patch of dirt or sand.

**Beckon Fire.** You create a thin cloud of harmless embers and colored, scented smoke in a 5-foot Cube. You choose the color and scent, and the embers can light candles, torches, or lamps in that area. The smoke’s scent lingers for 1 minute.

**Beckon Water.** You create a spray of cool mist that lightly dampens creatures and objects in a 5-foot Cube. Alternatively, you create 1 cup of clean water either in an open container or on a surface, and the water evaporates in 1 minute.

**Sculpt Element.** You cause dirt, sand, fire, smoke, mist, or water that can fit in a 1-foot Cube to assume a crude shape (such as that of a creature) for 1 hour.

name: Fire Bolt
description: You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or an object within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d10 Fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell starts burning if it isn’t being worn or carried.

name: Friends
description:
You magically emanate a sense of friendship toward one creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. The target succeeds automatically if it isn’t a Humanoid, if you’re fighting it, or if you have cast this spell on it within the past 24 hours.

The spell ends early if the target takes damage or if you make an attack roll, deal damage, or force anyone to make a saving throw. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

name: Guidance
description: You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. Until the spell ends, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using the chosen skill.

name: Light
description:
You touch one Large or smaller object that isn’t being worn or carried by someone else. Until the spell ends, the object sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The light can be colored as you like.

Covering the object with something opaque blocks the light. The spell ends if you cast it again.

name: Mage Hand
description:
A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again.

When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

As a Magic action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet.

The hand can’t attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

name: Mending
description:
This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin. As long as the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage.

This spell can physically repair a magic item, but it can’t restore magic to such an object.

name: Message
description:
You point toward a creature within range and whisper a message. The target (and only the target) hears the message and can reply in a whisper that only you can hear.

You can cast this spell through solid objects if you are familiar with the target and know it is beyond the barrier. Magical silence; 1 foot of stone, metal, or wood; or a thin sheet of lead blocks the spell.

name: Mind Sliver
description: You try to temporarily sliver the mind of one creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on an DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Intelligence saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Psychic damage and subtract 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.

name: Minor Illusion
description:
You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. See the descriptions below for the effects of each. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again.

If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save). If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.

**Sound.** If you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. It can be your voice, someone else’s voice, a lion’s roar, a beating of drums, or any other sound you choose. The sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends.

**Image.** If you create an image of an object—such as a chair, muddy footprints, or a small chest—it must be no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image can’t create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it.

name: Poison Spray
description: You spray toxic mist at a creature within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d12 Poison damage.

name: Prestidigitation
description:
You create a magical effect within range. Choose the effect from the options below. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its non-instantaneous effects active at a time.

**Sensory Effect.** You create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, faint musical notes, or an odd odor.

**Fire Play.** You instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire.

**Clean or Soil.** You instantaneously clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot.

**Minor Sensation.** You chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material for 1 hour.

**Magic Mark.** You make a color, a small mark, or a symbol appear on an object or a surface for 1 hour.

**Minor Creation.** You create a nonmagical trinket or an illusory image that can fit in your hand. It lasts until the end of your next turn. A trinket can deal no damage and has no monetary worth.

name: Produce Flame
description:
A flickering flame appears in your hand and remains there for the duration. While there, the flame emits no heat and ignites nothing, and it sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The spell ends if you cast it again.

Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to hurl fire at a creature or an object within 60 feet of you. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Fire damage.

name: Ray of Frost
description: A frigid beam of blue-white light streaks toward a creature within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, it takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Cold damage, and its Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

name: Resistance
description: You touch a willing creature and choose a damage type: Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Piercing, Poison, Radiant, Slashing, or Thunder. When the creature takes damage of the chosen type before the spell ends, the creature reduces the total damage taken by 1d4. A creature can benefit from this spell only once per turn.

name: Sacred Flame
description: Flame-like radiance descends on a creature that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Radiant damage. The target gains no benefit from Half Cover or Three-Quarters Cover for this save.

name: Shillelagh
description:
A Club or Quarterstaff you are holding is imbued with nature's power. For the duration, you make attack rolls with it with a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) modifier and deal 1d8$(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.bonus)) Force damage.

The spell ends early if you cast it again or if you let go of the weapon.

name: Shocking Grasp
description: Lightning springs from you to a creature that you try to touch. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Lightning damage, and it can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

name: Sorcerous Burst
description:
You cast sorcerous energy at one creature or object within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 damage of a type you choose: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Psychic, or Thunder.

If you roll an 8 on a d8 for this spell, you can roll another d8, and add it to the damage. When you cast this spell, the maximum number of these d8s you can add to the spell’s damage equals $(attributes.spellcasting.bonus).

name: Spare the Dying
description: Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and isn’t dead. The creature becomes Stable.

name: Starry Wisp
description: You launch a mote of light at one creature or object within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Radiant damage, and until the end of your next turn, it emits Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and can’t benefit from the Invisible condition.

name: Thaumaturgy
description:
You manifest a minor wonder within range. You create one of the effects below within range. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its 1-minute effects active at a time.

**Altered Eyes.** You alter the appearance of your eyes for 1 minute.

**Booming Voice.** Your voice booms up to three times as loud as normal for 1 minute. For the duration, you have Advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

**Fire Play.** You cause flames to flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute.

**Invisible Hand.** You instantaneously cause an unlocked door or window to fly open or slam shut.

**Phantom Sound.** You create an instantaneous sound that originates from a point of your choice within range, such as a rumble of thunder, the cry of a raven, or ominous whispers.

**Tremors.** You cause harmless tremors in the ground for 1 minute.

name: Thorn Whip
description: You create a vine-like whip covered in thorns that lashes out at your command toward a creature in range. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Piercing damage, and if it is Large or smaller, you can pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.

name: Thunderclap
description: Each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Thunder damage. The spell’s thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away.

name: Toll the Dead
description: You point at one creature you can see within range, and the single chime of a dolorous bell is audible within 10 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d8 Necrotic damage. If the target is missing any of its Hit Points, it instead takes 1d12 Necrotic damage.

name: True Strike
description: Guided by a flash of magical insight, you make one attack with the weapon used in the spell's casting. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) attack roll. On a hit, deal your weapon's damage die $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.bonus)) Radiant damage.

name: Vicious Mockery
description: You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at one creature you can see or hear within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

name: Word of Radiance
description: Burning radiance erupts from you in a 5-foot Emanation. Each creature of your choice that you can see in it must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw or take $(meta.level >= 17 ? 4 : meta.level >= 11 ? 3 : meta.level >= 5 ? 2 : 1)d6 Radiant damage.

name: Alarm
description:
You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won’t set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is audible or mental:

**Audible Alarm.** The alarm produces the sound of a handbell for 10 seconds within 60 feet of the warded area.

**Mental Alarm.** You are alerted by a mental ping if you are within 1 mile of the warded area. This ping awakens you if you’re asleep.

name: Animal Friendship
description: Target a Beast that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. If you or one of your allies deals damage to the target, the spells ends.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You can target one additional Beast for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Eldritch Armor
description: Protective magical frost surrounds you. You gain 5 Temporary Hit Points. If a creature hits you with a melee attack roll before the spell ends, the creature takes 5 Cold damage. The spell ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The Temporary Hit Points and the Cold damage both increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Eldritch Arms
description: Invoking Hadar, you cause tendrils to erupt from yourself. Each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 2d6 Necrotic damage and can’t take Reactions until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Bane
description: Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must each make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Charisma saving throw. Whenever a target that fails this save makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must subtract 1d4 from the attack roll or save.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Bless
description: You bless up to three creatures within range. Whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target adds 1d4 to the attack roll or save.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Burning Hands
description:
A thin sheet of flames shoots forth from you. Each creature in a 15-foot Cone makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Flammable objects in the Cone that aren’t being worn or carried start burning.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Charm Person
description: One Humanoid you can see within range makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Chromatic Orb
description:
You hurl an orb of energy at a target within range. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder for the type of orb you create, and then make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 damage of the chosen type.

If you roll the same number on two or more of the d8s, the orb leaps to a different target of your choice within 30 feet of the target. Make an attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. The orb can’t leap again unless you cast the spell with a level 2+ spell slot.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1. The orb can leap a maximum number of times equal to the level of the slot expended, and a creature can be targeted only once by each casting of this spell.

name: Color Spray
description: You launch a dazzling array of flashing, colorful light. Each creature in a 15-foot Cone originating from you must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Constitution saving throw or have the Blinded condition until the end of your next turn.

name: Command
description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:

**Approach.** The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

**Drop.** The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

**Flee.** The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

**Grovel.** The target has the Prone condition and then ends its turn.

**Halt.** On its turn, the target doesn’t move and takes no action or Bonus Action.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Compelled Duel
description:
You try to compel a creature into a duel. One creature that you can see within range makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and it can't willingly move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you.

The spell ends if you make an attack roll against a creature other than the target, if you cast a spell on an enemy other than the target, if an ally of yours damages the target, or if you end your turn more than 30 feet away from the target.

name: Comprehend Languages
description: For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any language that you hear or see signed. You also understand any written language that you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written. It takes about 1 minute to read one page of text. This spell doesn't decode symbols or secret messages.

name: Create or Destroy Water
description:
You do one of the following:

**Create Water.** You create up to 10 gallons of clean water within range in an open container. Alternatively, the water falls as rain in a 30-foot Cube within range, extinguishing exposed flames there.

**Destroy Water.** You destroy up to 10 gallons of water in an open container within range. Alternatively, you destroy fog in a 30-foot Cube within range.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You create or destroy 10 additional gallons of water, or the size of the Cube increases by 5 feet, for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Cure Wounds
description: A creature you touch regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8$(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.bonus)).

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The healing increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Detect Evil and Good
description:
For the duration, you sense the location of any Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead within 30 feet of yourself. You also sense whether the Hallow spell is active there and, if so, where.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

name: Detect Magic
description:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the Magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's school of magic.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

name: Detect Poison and Disease
description:
For the duration, you sense the location of poisons, poisonous or venomous creatures, and magical contagions within 30 feet of yourself. You sense the kind of poison, creature, or contagion in each case.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

name: Disguise Self
description:
You make yourself - including your clothing, armor, weapons, and other belongings on your person - look different until the spell ends. You can seem 1 foot shorter or taller and can appear heavier or lighter. You must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs as you have. Otherwise, the extent of the illusion is up to you.

The changes wrought by this spell fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, if you use this spell to add a hat to your outfit, objects pass through the hat, and anyone who touches it would feel nothing.

To discern that you are disguised, a creature must take the Study action to inspect your appearance and succeed on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save).

name: Dissonant Whispers
description: One creature of your choice that you can see within range hears a discordant melody in its mind. The target makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Psychic damage and must immediately use its Reaction, if available, to move as far away from you as it can, using the safest route. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Divine Favor
description: Until the spell ends, your attacks with weapons deal an extra 1d4 Radiant damage on a hit.

name: Divine Smite
description: Immediately after hitting a target with a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra 2d8 Radiant damage from the attack. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is a Fiend or an Undead.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Ensnaring Strike
description:
Immediately after hitting a creature with a weapon, grasping vines appear on it, and it makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw. A Large or larger creature has Advantage on this save. On a failed save, the target has the Restrained condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the vines shrivel away, and the spell ends.

While Restrained, the target takes 1d6 Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save). On a success, the spell ends.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Entangle
description:
Grasping plants sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. For the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into Difficult Terrain. They disappear when the spell ends.

Each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition until the spell ends. A Restrained creature can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save). On a success, it frees itself from the grasping plants and is no longer Restrained by them.

name: Expeditious Retreat
description: You take the Dash action, and until the spell ends, you can take that action again as a Bonus Action.

name: Faerie Fire
description:
Objects in a 20-foot Cube within range are outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Each creature in the Cube is also outlined if it fails a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and can’t benefit from the Invisible condition.

Attack rolls against an affected creature or object have Advantage if the attacker can see it.

name: False Life
description: You gain 2d4+4 Temporary Hit Points.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: You gain 5 additional Temporary Hit Points for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Feather Fall
description: Immediately after you or a creature you can see within 60 feet of you falls, choose up to five falling creatures within range. A falling creature’s rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until the spell ends. If a creature lands before the spell ends, the creature takes no damage from the fall, and the spell ends for that creature.

name: Fog Cloud
description: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The Sphere is Heavily Obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The fog’s radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Goodberry
description:
Ten berries appear in your hand and are infused with magic for the duration. A creature can take a Bonus Action to eat one berry. Eating a berry restores 1 Hit Point, and the berry provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day.

Uneaten berries disappear when the spell ends.

name: Grease
description:
Nonflammable grease covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within range and turns it into Difficult Terrain for the duration.

When the grease appears, each creature standing in its area must succeed on a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition. A creature that enters the area or ends its turn there must also succeed on that save or fall Prone.

name: Guiding Bolt
description: You hurl a bolt of light toward a creature within range. Make a $(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.attack)) ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, it takes 4d6 Radiant damage, and the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn has Advantage.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Hail of Thorns
description: Immediately after hitting a creature with a Ranged weapon, this spell creates a rain of thorns that sprouts from your Ranged weapon or ammunition. The target of the attack and each creature within 5 feet of it make a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw, taking 1d10 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Healing Word
description: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4$(formatBonus(attributes.spellcasting.bonus)).

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Hellish Rebuke
description: Immediately after taking damage from a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself, the creature that damaged you is momentarily surrounded by green flames. It makes a DC $(attributes.spellcasting.save) Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
extra.description: The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

name: Heroism
description: A willing creature you touch is imbued with bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is immune to the Frightened condition and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to $(attributes.spellcasting.bonus) at the start of each of its turns.

extra.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
